subject: people who married their high school sweetheart
tagline: Elastic for people who married their high school sweetheart
one_liner: Full-text search and uptime monitoring for the one relationship you've run since the eleventh grade.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan enterprise-infra pitch. Treats a single decades-long marriage as a production system: indexed, observable, alertable. The absurdity is played completely straight, like a Series C observability company that genuinely believes your relationship needs an SLA. No winks. Mono telemetry numbers. The romance is never mentioned warmly, only measured.

hero.sub: Tenure ingests every text, anniversary, and argument since 2008 and makes the whole marriage searchable in milliseconds. Uptime dashboards. Latency on your apologies. An alert when the person you've known since homeroom says something they've never said before.

features.title: Full-text marriage search
features.blurb: Every text since the flip phone, every anniversary card, every disagreement about the thermostat. Indexed, ranked, and searchable. Settle "I never said that" in 40 milliseconds, with the original document attached and timestamped.

features.title: Apology latency dashboards
features.blurb: Observability for the relationship you already have. Track p99 apology latency, anniversary recall rate, and a rolling sentiment score. Put an SLA on the silent treatment and get paged the moment you breach it.

features.title: One-person anomaly detection
features.blurb: A model trained on eighteen years of exactly one person. It flags the instant they use a word they have never used. When "fine" arrives in a tone the model has never logged, the alert fires before they finish the sentence.

pricing_tiers.name: Single Node
pricing_tiers.tagline: One relationship. No replication. If it goes down, it goes down.

pricing_tiers.name: High Availability
pricing_tiers.tagline: For couples who refuse to fail over.
perks: Unlimited retention since the eleventh grade

pricing_tiers.name: Enterprise · Renewal of Vows
pricing_tiers.tagline: A dedicated cluster for a relationship that is, frankly, load-bearing.
perks: Quarterly business review with both sets of in-laws

testimonials.quote: We've been together since sophomore homeroom, so I figured there was nothing left to find. Tenure surfaced a 2011 text where I agreed to host every Thanksgiving forever. I'd have argued. Now I host.
testimonials.name: Dale Pruitt
testimonials.title: Married His Lab Partner, 19 Years (Dayton, OH)

testimonials.quote: The dashboard told me my average apology latency was four days. I did not enjoy seeing that rendered on a chart in front of me. It has since come down.
testimonials.name: Sandra Voss-Eakin
testimonials.title: Prom Date, Then Wife, Then Co-Signer

testimonials.quote: It paged me at 6:14am because she said "sure" in a tone the model had never logged. The model was correct. Worth every dollar of the High Availability tier.
testimonials.name: Marcus Ngata
testimonials.title: High School Sweetheart, Now Also Emergency Contact

demo_screen_concept: A search-and-observability console for one marriage, indexed as 'marriage-2008'. A large query bar runs full-text search over the relationship; results return as ranked 'documents' (texts, arguments, anniversaries, promises) each with a timestamp, relevance score, and source tag. A facet sidebar filters by year, by person (You / Them), and by document type (argument, affection, promise, logistics). A right-hand observability rail shows live tabular-mono metrics: uptime in years, p99 apology latency, anniversary recall rate, current sentiment, and a small sparkline. Clicking a result expands the full document inline. All data is mocked client-side.

images.rationale: Typographic neo-brutalist hero (the oversized Lexend Mega wordmark and a mono console panel are the hero) skipped imagery. The demo is a search-and-observability console (terminal/document-shaped), so it gets no photo assets. Full budget went to the always-applicable fallback: three deadpan corporate testimonial headshots of ordinary people who married their high school sweetheart, each shot on a flat grey studio backdrop so it sits cleanly inside the brutalist black borders. One of four image slots unused.

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of an ordinary middle-aged white man in his early forties, short receding sandy hair, plain crew-neck sweater, a simple gold wedding band faintly visible, neutral unsmiling expression, looking directly into the camera. Even soft studio lighting, flat warm light-grey seamless studio backdrop, slightly desaturated documentary color, sharp focus, shallow depth of field. Sincere and unglamorous, like an enterprise software company's leadership directory photo. One focal subject only. Real photograph, not illustrated. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of an ordinary woman in her mid-forties, shoulder-length brown hair with a few greys, plain solid-color blouse, a simple wedding ring, calm neutral expression, direct eye contact with the camera. Even soft studio lighting, flat pale slate-grey seamless studio backdrop, slightly desaturated documentary color, sharp focus, shallow depth of field. Sincere and unglamorous, like a B2B software company's about page portrait. One focal subject only. Real photograph, not illustrated. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of an ordinary man in his early forties with brown skin, short dark hair and a neatly trimmed beard, plain collared shirt, a simple wedding band, neutral composed expression, looking straight into the camera. Even soft studio lighting, flat light-grey seamless studio backdrop, slightly desaturated documentary color, sharp focus, shallow depth of field. Sincere, corporate, unflashy, like an enterprise leadership directory photo. One focal subject only. Real photograph, not illustrated. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI.

design_direction.style: Neo-brutalist developer-infrastructure SaaS, light high-contrast mode. A stark paper-white ground carries hard-edged blocks outlined in 3px ink-black borders, each sitting on a solid offset drop-shadow (no blur, no gradient) that collapses on hover as the block nudges down-right into its own shadow. Two loud flat accents do all the signaling: an electric index-blue carries brand, structure, links, and the observability figures; a hot pink is rationed hard for the single primary CTA, the live 'love' affordance, and breach alerts, and appears almost nowhere else. A butter-yellow highlight field is used sparingly behind a single callout block. An oversized Lexend Mega wordmark and headlines do the heavy lifting in tight uppercase; Public Sans carries body copy. Every figure that pretends to be telemetry (uptime years, apology latency, recall rate, sentiment, prices) is set in JetBrains Mono with tabular figures, on purpose, to read like an observability console and not a love letter. Zero blur, zero gradients, zero soft rounded marketing cards, zero glassmorphism. Reads like a well-funded observability platform that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at a single marriage that started in the eleventh grade.

fonts.body: 'Public Sans', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Developer-infrastructure / observability SaaS landing in neo-brutalist style: a hard-bordered hero with an oversized wordmark and a mono uptime/stat block, three feature blocks as bordered cards with hard offset shadows, a butter-yellow callout strip, three testimonial quote blocks with bordered headshots, a three-tier pricing rack styled as cluster plans, and a demo handoff to the marriage search-and-observability console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Hard and snappy, not soft. Cards and buttons translate (2-6px down-right) on hover while their solid offset shadow collapses to 0, a 90ms step transition with no easing softness. Anchor links smooth-scroll. One restrained marquee strip of index terms. Respects prefers-reduced-motion by disabling the translate.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left: an enormous Lexend Mega uppercase wordmark 'TENURE' over the h1 and a tight sub, with the hot-pink 'Index your marriage' CTA and a black ghost 'open the demo' button. Right: a hard-bordered console card titled marriage-2008 showing mono telemetry rows (uptime 18y 4m, p99 apology latency, anniversary recall rate, sentiment) over a tiny sparkline. Faint engineering-dot grid behind everything. No photography in the hero; type and the mono panel are the hero.
